references.title: Stable Knowledge Tracing Using Causal Inference

extracted.Students: 4417 ASSISTments2009 learners, 19917 ASSISTments2012 learners, 333 Statics Learners, 2000 Simulated Learners
extracted.Flaw of Previous Models: Current KT models have wayu too oversimplistic assumptions, which makes them suffer from volatility:  Among thosse assumptions we have:
extracted.Novelty of Model:
Authors introduce a method that uses causal inference to predict outcome via knowledge graphs.

This is achieved by the additon of an autoencoder model.
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
The proposed model relies on a set of assumptions:

1- Stable Knowledge Concept Value - Ther is a stable influence that one knowledge concept can have in another.

2. Unconfoundedness - Knowledge concept layout and outcomes are conditionally independent on the data.

3. Separateness: there are 3 sets of variables: Confounding variables (X), Adjustment Variables (Z) and Irrelevant Variables (I)
extracted.Models: BKT, PFA, BKT+FSA, DKVMN, DKT, DKT+, Causal Inference KT
extracted.Comments:
The main distinction between this model and traditional DKT is the inclusion of restrictions of causal inference that enrich the feature set. 

Results show that, indeed, the inclusion of these restrictions make predictions a lot more stable than standard approaches in real datasets.
extracted.Limitations:
Does not seem to be effective in smaller sequences of answers - highlighted by the performance on the Synthetic dataset.

No measurement of additional data sources.

annotations.text: KT is a popular application of LA research and, nowadays, there are increasingly more student representations who are becoming increasingly complex. However, most approaches are inneficient at updating the learner’s knowledge state, often leading to poor predictions.

The authors employ a causal inference model to explain KT. The idea stems from the construcion of balanced weights for the different features. Experimentation on different datasets shows that model is accurate and interpretable (2 hallmarks for good KT models.
